title: derris lushaiensis
intro: Derris lushaiensis, also known as the Mizo derris or Mizo rosin root, is a perennial plant native to the northeastern regions of India. The plant is a member of the Fabaceae family and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots of Derris lushaiensis have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or powder, and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
